assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements prepared under IFRS.
The item being considered is an intragroup dividend and a related intragroup receivable/payable that has been recognised before consolidation eliminations.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 provides the top-level hedge accounting model and specifically addresses when items in consolidated financial statements can be designated as hedged items, including certain intragroup foreign currency exposures.

approaches.label: Consolidation elimination accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 and IAS 24 require full elimination of intragroup assets, liabilities, income, expenses and cash flows in consolidated financial statements, which is a distinct top-level accounting treatment directly relevant to intragroup dividends and receivables.
